items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23 (tech lead pass 2 — failure root cause: prior steps referenced non-existent test files and omitted required import change). PRECISE STEPS: (1) Read credentials.ts lines 10-13 — FILE_MODE_SECRET is imported but DIR_MODE_SECRET is NOT. (2) Edit credentials.ts line 13: change 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' to 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET }'. (3) Edit credentials.ts line 53: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (4) Read snapshot-registry.ts lines 1-15 — no import from config/defaults.js exists at all. (5) Add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js' to snapshot-registry.ts after the existing type import on line 13. (6) Edit snapshot-registry.ts line 51: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (7) Run npm test — full suite passes (no dedicated test file exists for credentials.ts or snapshot-registry.ts; full suite confirms no regressions). PO NOTE: AC3 says snapshots.json stays 'unchanged at 0600' but snapshot-registry.ts currently writes it without any mode flag (line 59: writeFileSync(tmpPath, content)) — it is not at 0600 today. AC3 is satisfiable only for credentials.json. The dir mode fix does not change file permissions. Flag to PO for AC correction if needed.

items.title: Capability rollback snapshot directories created without mode 0700 — integration credential backups world-listable
items.intent: ops/rollback/ subdirectories must be created with mode 0700 — integrations kind snapshots copy engine/credentials.json and cannot be world-listable.
items.description: src/evolve/rollback/capability-snapshot.ts creates ops/rollback/{kind}/ directories without mode 0700. Three call sites: line 80 (saveSnapshots: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })), line 100 (createCapabilitySnapshot: mkdirSync(snapshotDir, { recursive: true })), line 111 (target subdir: mkdirSync(destDir, { recursive: true })). The integrations kind snapshots engine/credentials.json (SNAPSHOT_TARGETS constant). World-listable rollback directory exposes that credential backups exist and reveals snapshot IDs. Fix: (1) Import DIR_MODE_SECRET from ../../config/defaults.js — no import exists there yet. (2) Apply { mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET } to all three mkdirSync calls at lines 80, 100, and 111.
steps: Read src/evolve/rollback/capability-snapshot.ts lines 1-25 — confirm no import from config/defaults.js exists
Add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js' after the existing type import on line 22
Read lines 74-115 — confirm three mkdirSync calls without mode at lines 80, 100, and 111
Edit line 80 (saveSnapshots): change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Edit line 100 (createCapabilitySnapshot snapshotDir): change mkdirSync(snapshotDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(snapshotDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Edit line 111 (target destDir): change mkdirSync(destDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(destDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Run npm test — confirm all tests pass (no dedicated test file for this module)
acceptanceCriteria: ops/rollback/{kind}/ directories are created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
Individual snapshot subdirectories are created with mode 0700
All existing tests pass with no regressions
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. All three mkdirSync calls are in one file (capability-snapshot.ts). The critical path is integrations kind which snapshots engine/credentials.json per SNAPSHOT_TARGETS. Read the file to confirm line numbers before editing. DIR_MODE_SECRET is not imported — add it. Apply mode to all three calls. No dedicated test file for capability-snapshot.ts — run npm test for full suite.

items.title: Backup restore creates deployment root and ops/ directory without mode 0700 — restored agent data world-listable
items.intent: When restore creates the deployment root or ops/ directory they must be created with mode 0700 — the restore path must not undermine scaffold hardening.
items.description: src/operate/backup/restore.ts has two mkdirSync calls without mode 0700: line 217 (mkdirSync(deployDir, { recursive: true })) creates the root ~/.clawhq/ directory, and line 299 (mkdirSync(destOps, { recursive: true })) creates the ops/ subdirectory during restore. scaffold.ts (fixed by BUG-084) creates these directories with 0700, but the restore code path has independent mkdirSync calls that create them at 0755 if they do not already exist. This means a fresh restore on a new system bypasses scaffold hardening.
steps: Read src/operate/backup/restore.ts lines 1-20 — confirm whether DIR_MODE_SECRET is already imported from config/defaults.js
Add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js' if not already present
Read restore.ts line 217 — confirm mkdirSync(deployDir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Edit line 217: change mkdirSync(deployDir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(deployDir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Read restore.ts line 299 (copyOpsSelectively function) — confirm mkdirSync(destOps, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Edit line 299: change mkdirSync(destOps, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(destOps, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Run npm test — confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: Restore creates deployDir with mode 0700 when directory does not already exist
Restore creates destOps (ops/ subdirectory) with mode 0700 when directory does not already exist
All existing tests pass with no regressions
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Two isolated mkdirSync calls in restore.ts — one for the deployment root, one in copyOpsSelectively. Both need DIR_MODE_SECRET. Check whether restore.ts already imports from defaults.js before adding import. No dedicated restore test file may exist — run full suite.

items.title: workspace/memory/ directory created without mode 0700 — approval queue directory world-listable
items.intent: workspace/memory/ must be created with mode 0700 — the directory must not be world-listable regardless of file permissions inside it.
items.description: Two code paths create workspace/memory/ without mode 0700. (1) src/evolve/approval/queue.ts line 58 (saveQueue function: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })). (2) src/design/tools/approve-action.ts line 61 in the generated script template (mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })). BUG-090 fixed approval-queue.json file permissions to 0600, but the directory remains world-listable. Fix: (1) In queue.ts, import DIR_MODE_SECRET from config/defaults.js and pass { mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET } to mkdirSync. (2) In approve-action.ts generated template string, update the mkdirSync literal to include mode: 0o700.
steps: Read src/evolve/approval/queue.ts lines 1-20 — confirm no import from config/defaults.js exists
Add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from ../../config/defaults.js' to queue.ts imports
Read queue.ts line 58 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Edit queue.ts line 58: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET })
Read src/design/tools/approve-action.ts line 61 — this is inside a template string; find mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) in the generated script

Edit approve-action.ts line 61: change the template string literal mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 }) — note: uses literal 0o700 not DIR_MODE_SECRET since this is a generated CJS script
Run npm test — confirm all tests pass
acceptanceCriteria: workspace/memory/ directory is created with mode 0700 via queue.ts saveQueue path
Generated approve-action script creates workspace/memory/ with mode 0700
approval-queue.json file permissions remain unchanged at 0600
All existing tests pass with no regressions
items.groomingNotes: GROOMED 2026-03-23. Two call sites: queue.ts (TypeScript, use DIR_MODE_SECRET) and approve-action.ts template string (generated CommonJS script, must use literal 0o700 not the imported constant). BUG-090 is the parent fix for this file — this closes the directory half that BUG-090 left open.
